title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: A professional is decorating our living room at the moment.
solutions: are having our living room decorated

questions.firstSentence: ‘Don't forget to lock the door,’ said my mother.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with object + to-infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: I really regret not buying that house when I had the chance.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect for past regret.
questions.secondSentence: I (1) _________ that house when I had the chance.

questions.firstSentence: It's possible that they forgot about the meeting.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for past possibility.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't realize who she was until she started speaking.
questions.explanation: Tests 'It was only when...' cleft sentences.
questions.secondSentence: It (1) _________ she started speaking that I realized who she was.

questions.firstSentence: I find it difficult to get up early in the morning.
questions.explanation: Tests 'be used to' + gerund for familiarity.
questions.secondSentence: I am (1) _________ up early in the morning.
